title: Tags, Versions, Releases, and Provenance · Repository Trace: Lightweight Annotated Tags
summary: Prepare a release candidate whose source identity, version, evidence, and published reference can be independently verified. Planned artifact: Add and verify the git lightweight annotated tags increment in a signed-off release evidence record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish lightweight and annotated tag objects, messages, taggers, targets, and intended use.
Prevent and respond to secrets, large binaries, generated artifacts, and sensitive history with policy and rotation boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Release v3.1.0 targets commit a11ce00, but the working tree has an unstaged version edit. Establish source identity, clean state, annotated tag evidence, checksum, and trust limits. A defensible solution applies Lightweight Annotated Tags, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a signed-off release evidence record.

content.code:
git status --porcelain=v1
git show --no-patch --format=fuller HEAD
git tag -a "v2.1.0" -m 'Verified release candidate' HEAD
git show --no-patch "v2.1.0^{commit}"
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
